zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Maud de Nerford於1285年出生在英國諾福爾可斯吉頓時，她的父親William de Nerford36歲，母親Petronilla de Vaux30歲。她與John de Warrene結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1345年在英國爾波易頓享年60歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭素可列維斯。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Nerford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: habitational name from a place of that name in Westphalia.

variants.name: Hereford
meanings: English: habitational name from Hertford (Hertfordshire) (in which the t was lost early, as in the modern pronunciation Harford), Hereford (Herefordshire), or Harford (Devon, Gloucestershire). The Devon and Herefordshire placenames derive from Old English here ‘army’ + ford ‘ford’, while the Gloucestershire and Hertfordshire placenames derive from Old English heorot ‘hart, stag’ + ford.

nameMeanings.name: Lady
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English lavedi, levedi, ladi ‘lady, female head of a household, woman, wife’ (Old English hlǣfdige). As a man's name it may have been used in mockery, or be short for occupational names such as Ladyman.
Hungarian (Ládi): habitational name for someone from Lád in Borsod County or Lad in Somogy County.

variants.name: Lada
meanings: ‘tendril’ or ‘creeper’, often used in metaphors describing the slender curvature of eyebrows, arms, hair, swords, lightning, and women's bodies.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Maud de Nerford was born in 1285, in Skeyton, Norfolk, England, her father, William de Nerford, was 36 and her mother, Petronilla de Vaux, was 30. She married John de Warrene. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1345, in Poynton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 60, and was buried in Lewes, Sussex, England, United Kingdom.

Elle a épousé John de Warrene. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1345, à Poynton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans, et a été enterrée à Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Maud de Nerford(이)가 1285년 영국 노르폴크 스케이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William de Nerford(은)는 36세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Petronilla de Vaux(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 John de Warrene(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1345년 영국 체실르 포이턴에서 60세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 루스에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Maud de Nerford родилась в 1285 г. в местности скитон, норфолк, Англия, её отцу по имени William de Nerford было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Petronilla de Vaux, 30. Она умерла в 1345 г. в местности поинтон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет и была похоронена в местности львесь, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания.
